Alternate Title Data

The region hierarchy system on the Xbox isolates title data so that titles can safely access their own data without worrying about damaging or destroying the data of other titles. However, it is often useful and even quite desirable for one title to share data with another title. For example, a sequel title could use saved characters from the original game, or a group of titles could share custom user settings rather than have the user set them in each game. In order to enable this functionality, titles can, under certain conditions, access the user data or title data regions of another title.

Access to a title's data by other games is restricted and cannot occur if the game publisher does not desire it. A game can access another title's data only if either the two games have the same publisher, or the title to be mapped is listed in the mapping title's certificate as a valid alternate title.

You access a related title's data by mounting that title's data region with the XMountAlternateTitle function. The function maps the other title's user data or persistent data region to a logical drive letter, and returns that drive letter for use by the mapping title.

When you are done with the other title's data, call XUnmountAlternateTitle to dismount the mapped region.

Only one alternate title's user data region can be mounted at a time. To find all of the potential user data of another title, you must mount and dismount, in turn, the alternate title's user data region first on the hard disk, and then on every connected memory unit.
